terms.label: Hidden causal rule
terms.definition: The story contains a stable but initially unknown rule whose discovery can change action.

terms.label: Revisited place
terms.definition: The dramatic spine returns to a place after knowledge, capability, allegiance, or world state changes.

terms.label: Parallel state
terms.definition: One place or relationship has two meaningfully contrasting states the player can compare.

terms.label: Schedule pressure
terms.definition: Character routines or repeating time windows make observation and timing narratively meaningful.

terms.label: Transformable environment
terms.definition: Physical or social surroundings can change persistently in response to a player-understood rule.

terms.label: Multi-causal problem
terms.definition: A dramatic obstacle admits several coherent interventions rather than one prescribed answer.

terms.label: Landmark mystery
terms.definition: A visible place or object can promise future meaning before the player can fully reach or use it.

terms.label: Relationship memory
terms.definition: An NPC relationship can remember action, knowledge, trust, or failure across encounters.

terms.label: Capability reveal
terms.definition: A learned action can express character change while opening new spatial or narrative possibilities.

terms.label: Embodied secret
terms.definition: A reveal is stronger when enacted or witnessed spatially than when delivered as exposition.

terms.label: Confined stage
terms.definition: The story has few locations, so depth must come from state, perspective, schedule, and re-use.

terms.label: Low interaction fit
terms.definition: The source depends on uninterrupted language, fixed chronology, or unchangeable space that resists systemic play.

governance.changePolicy: Signals require a documented story-model path and positive plus negative benchmark coverage.
